rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWAN VS. DANIEL LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 20 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT DANIEL LOPEZ Motion For Order To Quash Service Of Summons. Denied. Plaintiff effectuated service on Defendant by publication, pursuant to the October 23, 2014 order. The evidence establishes that Plaintiff had not ascertained an address for Defendant at the time of publication and therefore, Plaintiff was not obligated to mail the summons and complaint. Plaintiff attempted to serve Defendant many times at the Irving Street address, but was unable to do so. Therefore, it was reasonable for Plaintiff to conclude that the Irving Street address was not a valid address for Defendant. Defendant shall file his answer to the First Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA CAVANAUGH VS. DOMINIC MASSONI CPA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 20 2015, Line 16, DEFENDANT DOMINIC MASSONI Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion denied. It is not necessary that a complete cause of action exist at the time of death in order for rights to survive that may later mature into an actionable claim. (Carr v. Progressive Cas. Ins. Co. (1984) 152 Cal.App.3d 881, 891.) In addition, whether, when, and to what extent the Decedent was injured is a fact issue not appropriate for resolution on the pleadings. Further, as pled, the causes of action are not barred by the statute of limitations. As determined on Defendant's previous Demurrer, the FAC adequately alleges delayed discovery. (FAC 14.) Defendant cites nothing in the prove-up papers that alters or undermines the relevant allegations in the FAC. Finally, the damages for attorneys' and accountants' fees incurred to correct and mitigate damages resulting from Defendant's alleged negligence are proper damages in a malpractice action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. VS. SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Unredacted Copies Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 20, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Unredacted Copies Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Please consider the following questions and issues in preparation for the hearing: (1) whether or not other member-entities believes Ms. Weissman acted as their counsel; (2) whether the dominant role Ms. Weissman played in her relationship with the other member-entities was a legal or business role; (3) the communications between Ms. Weissman and plaintiff. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: D CAMERON BAKER VS. PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant D. Cameron Bakers Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pwdf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 20 2015, Line 15, PLAINTIFF D BAKER Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant D. Cameron Bakers Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pwdf. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is granted. Defendant will produce all documents responsive to requests nos. 1-9 within ten (10) days from the date of this order. To the extent that defendant is withholding any responsive document based on a claim of privilege, it will describe the document with sufficient particularity to enable plaintiff to challenge the claim. In addition, if defendants asserts that it is unable to produce a document based upon the order of the U.S. District Court it shall describe that document and explain why it contents are covered by that order. The complaint seeks money damages for an alleged breach of a contract to pay a bonus in legal fees based upon recoveries in the actions filed in the U.S. District Court. All of the requested documents relate to that claim and should have been produced without controversy. Defendant was not justified in refusing to produce any document without a protective order. Monetary sanctions will be assessed in the amount of $3,000.00. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: Jones Memorial United Methodist vs. Jones Memorial Homes, etal
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 20 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC., JONES SENIOR HOMES, INC., GERALD JONES, EMMETT COBB, CHARLES HARRIS, ROZELLE LEE, JAMES MCCARY, MARVIN NORMAN, TOLIVER, DEREK (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence indicates that defendant Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. is an entity independent from plaintiff and Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. amended its by-laws to become a non-member entity. Defendants correctly cite the HUD regulatory agreement that required HUD approval only for changes to the by-laws "with reference to the purposes of the corporation or the disposition of the assets upon dissolution." HUD did not need to approve membership changes. The change in the by-laws does not conflict with Jones Memorial Homes, Inc.'s Articles of Incorporation. See Health Maintenance Network v. Blue Cross (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 1043, 1059. In addition, any conceivable statute of limitations bars plaintiff's claims. The declaration of Erma Cobb demonstrates that plaintiff was on inquiry notice regarding the issues raised in plaintiff's First Amended Complaint in March of 2007. Plaintiff filed this untimely action on March 21, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK VS. ANTONIO MA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 20 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication. The matter is continued to March 19, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 20, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CATHY CHEUNG VS. JENNY PHUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, PERMANENT PROTECTIVE ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 20, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CHEUNG, CATHY, TRUSTEE OF THE SURVIVING SPOUSE MOTION FOR TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, PERMANENT PROTECTIVE ORDER Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Please consider the following questions and issues in preparation for the hearing: (1) the details of the parties' meet and confer efforts; (2) issues discussed or testimony in deposition that plaintiff believes require follow-up or clarification or is otherwise objectionable; (3) the specific timeline of events on the day of the deposition. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt
